title: Fix broken targeted cache invalidation for mutated entities
description:
Review finding:
Targeted invalidation cannot work for entity-scoped cache entries. Cache keys are SHA-256 hashes of the request payload, but invalidation tries substring matching on the raw entity id. As a result, update/delete/create flows leave stale GET responses in cache until TTL expiry.

Evidence:
- fastmcp_server/bookstack/tools.py:419-429 hashes cache keys
- fastmcp_server/bookstack/cache.py:96-108 invalidates by substring match
- fastmcp_server/bookstack/cache.py:172-187 passes str(entity_id) into invalidate()
- fastmcp_server/bookstack/tools.py:1899-1901 and 1922-1924 call targeted invalidation after mutations

Impact:
Clients can receive stale books/pages/images immediately after successful writes, which is a correctness bug and can hide state changes for up to the cache TTL.

Suggested fix:
Store structured cache metadata that includes entity ids/path prefixes, or invalidate the whole bucket on writes until precise key tracking exists. Add a regression test that proves a mutated entity is no longer served from cache.

title: Block SSRF targets in URL-based image uploads
description:
Review finding:
The URL upload path does not implement real SSRF protection. It only checks for an http/https scheme and a non-empty netloc, then performs requests.get() directly against the supplied URL. This still allows localhost, RFC1918/private IPs, link-local addresses, and cloud metadata endpoints.

Evidence:
- README.md:59-63 claims scheme restriction avoids SSRF
- fastmcp_server/bookstack/tools.py:623-628 only validates scheme + netloc
- fastmcp_server/bookstack/tools.py:648-668 fetches the URL directly with requests.get()
- fastmcp_server/bookstack/validators.py:116-142 already has localhost-aware URL validation logic, but it is not used here

Impact:
Any MCP client allowed to call image uploads can make the server reach internal network resources and potentially exfiltrate sensitive data through the upload path.

Suggested fix:
Reject localhost/private/link-local/reserved address ranges after DNS resolution, disable redirects to disallowed targets, and add tests for 127.0.0.1, ::1, RFC1918 ranges, and metadata IPs.

title: Reject unsupported MIME types for remote image uploads
description:
Review finding:
Remote image uploads do not actually enforce the documented MIME allowlist. When the response Content-Type is missing or unsupported and the filename guess also fails, the code silently falls back to application/octet-stream and proceeds with the upload instead of rejecting the payload.

Evidence:
- README.md:59-64 says MIME type is validated against accepted formats
- fastmcp_server/bookstack/tools.py:698-706 falls back to application/octet-stream instead of raising an error
- fastmcp_server/tests/test_manage_images.py currently asserts the weak fallback path instead of rejecting unsupported types

Impact:
The tool can forward non-image or ambiguously typed content to BookStack, which weakens input validation and makes behavior depend on downstream API rejection instead of local enforcement.

Suggested fix:
Fail closed when neither the response MIME type nor an extension-based guess lands in the allowlist, and replace the current fallback-oriented tests with rejection tests for unsupported content types.
